what_it_does: Documents a structural ledger of 49 gallery mockups, mapping each piece by category, group, dials, archetype, viewport shape, shared-system linkage, and visual look-signature as observed from source files.
observations: States its provenance: manifest data plus latest displayed mockup files, structural greps, targeted reads, and CSS root definitions.
Defines the shared design-system anchors as ui/piece.css, ui/phone.css, ui/organisms.js, and ui/unit-view.js, then records which pieces use or do not use those layers.
Uses a six-archetype taxonomy and gives counts that sum to 49, while noting that portrait phone is a component rather than a standalone top-level archetype.
Organizes the 49 pieces into Slides, Application, and Further Out, with detailed per-piece tables for ids, titles, dials, archetypes, viewports, linkage, and look-signatures.
Separates older inline or locally reimplemented scaffolds from pieces that link shared CSS and organism scripts.
Ends with a geometry summary that reduces the wall to landscape .view and portrait .screen outer canvas shapes.
standouts: The file is both an inventory and an audit of scaffold/chrome usage across the mockup wall.
It records observed linkage state alongside visual characterization, not only visual descriptions.
It treats ui/unit-view.js as a built presentation rung that no listed gallery piece consumes yet.
conventions: Uses markdown sections, explanatory prose, and dense tables rather than code symbols.
Marks evidence language explicitly with terms like Observed and Verified.
Keeps piece identities in backticked ids and groups them by manifest/category language.
Combines descriptive look-signatures with implementation details such as CSS classes, linked assets, and inline scaffolds.
concerns: Some entries include evaluative severity wording inside the source text, which may mix audit priority with neutral inventory.
The document is long and manually dense, so stale observations would be hard to detect from this file alone.
Several claims depend on external files and greps, but this file does not include machine-checkable query output or timestamps beyond its prose method.
notes: Briefing note: this file is the source-facing map of the 49 mockup wall, especially the relationship between visual archetypes, viewport geometry, and shared design-system adoption.
questions: Which exact manifest revision or commit was used for the stated 49-piece count?
What defines the latest displayed file when a piece has multiple versions?
Are the linkage observations intended to be regenerated automatically or maintained by manual review?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is a piece-by-piece structural map based on source files, with provenance, taxonomy, per-piece characterization, and design-system linkage notes.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make the gallery legible as a ledger of reusable substrates, showing how each mockup relates to shared CSS, organism furniture, viewport shapes, and future live composition.
novelty: Unusual as a design-system document because it maps visual mockups through file-level implementation evidence, archetypes, and shared-system linkage rather than screenshots alone.
contribution: Contributes a neutral ledger of mockup identities, archetypes, dimensions, dials, visual signatures, and implementation linkage states that a fused system would keep as descriptive metadata and provenance for the gallery wall.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown audit ledger for counterpart-design's 49 gallery mockups, cataloging each piece by category, archetype, viewport geometry, warmth/register dials, shared design-system usage, and source-observed look-signature.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to make the mockup wall structurally searchable and to expose how each piece relates to the shared design scaffold, phone chrome, organism furniture, and future renderer direction.
